message.content:
I need to compare how much ranking information is extracted per provider call for these two methods.

**A (single_scalar_rating):** Each call produces one score for one item on one attribute. To rank N items across K attributes, you need N×K calls. Each call gives a single scalar value. The information per call is minimal - just one number.

**B (whole_list_sort):** A single call returns a complete ranked ordering of all N items simultaneously, considering all attributes and weights at once. One call produces N-1 bits of ordering information (a full permutation), which scales with the number of items.

**Analysis:**
- Method A: Each call extracts one score for one item. To get a full ranking of N items, you need many calls. Information per call = 1 score.
- Method B: A single call extracts the entire ranked list. Information per call = full ordering of all N items = O(N log N) bits of information.

Method B extracts dramatically more ranking information per call. For even a modest list of 10 items, B extracts ~10x more ranking information per call. For larger lists (20-50 items), the ratio grows substantially.

The ratio depends on list size, but the structural advantage of B is clear and large. For typical use cases (10-50 items), B extracts roughly 10-26x more information per call. I'll estimate around 15x as a reasonable middle ground for typical ranking tasks.

```json
{"higher_id":"B","ratio":15.0,"confidence":0.82}
```
